seo.title: Recruitment/HR Code Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for code generation AI agents in recruitment/hr. Covers EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144, EU AI Act (high-risk).

content.intro: Deploying AI agents that generate code within recruitment and HR workflows introduces a dual layer of risk: the code itself must be safe, sandboxed, and free of security vulnerabilities, while the hiring logic it encodes must comply with EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144, and the EU AI Act's high-risk AI provisions. A single misconfigured scoring algorithm or biased feature weight embedded in generated code can trigger discriminatory outcomes at scale, exposing your organization to EEOC enforcement actions and fines up to $1,500 per violation. This checklist ensures every layer of your AI-generated recruitment code pipeline is auditable, bias-mitigated, and legally defensible.

sections.heading: Bias Prevention & Fair Hiring Compliance
sections.description: Ensure all code generated by the AI agent for resume screening, candidate scoring, or ranking explicitly avoids encoding discrimination against protected classes under EEOC, ADA, and state hiring bias laws.

items.label: Prohibit Protected Characteristic Inputs in Generated Code
items.description: Validate that AI-generated screening or scoring code does not accept, process, or weight inputs such as name, zip code (as a proxy for race), graduation year (age proxy), or gender pronouns. Implement static analysis rules that flag any variable referencing these fields.

items.label: Enforce Disparate Impact Testing on Generated Scoring Logic
items.description: Before deploying any AI-generated candidate ranking or filtering function, run automated disparate impact analysis (4/5ths rule) across race, gender, age, and disability status using synthetic or historical candidate data to confirm no protected group is disproportionately excluded.

items.label: Restrict Training Signal Sources in Generated ML Code
items.description: If the agent generates model training code, enforce a blocklist of historically biased features (e.g., prior employer prestige, university tier rankings) and require documentation of each feature's job-relatedness before inclusion.

items.label: Mandate Explainability Outputs in Scoring Functions
items.description: Require all AI-generated candidate scoring code to produce human-readable rationale outputs (e.g., top 3 factors per decision) so that HR reviewers can audit and override automated decisions, satisfying ADA reasonable accommodation review requirements.

items.label: Validate ADA Accommodation Logic in Assessment Code
items.description: Review any generated code that sets time limits, interface constraints, or skill thresholds for candidate assessments to ensure accommodation pathways (extended time, alternative formats) are programmatically available and not bypassed.

items.label: Log All Candidate Filtering Decisions with Immutable Audit Trail
items.description: Ensure generated code writes every filtering or scoring decision to an append-only audit log including timestamp, candidate ID (anonymized), decision outcome, and model version, retaining records for a minimum of 4 years per EEOC record-keeping requirements.

items.label: Block Use of Generative Text for Candidate Rejection Messaging
items.description: Prevent the AI agent from generating automated rejection email copy that could inadvertently reveal discriminatory criteria or violate state-specific adverse action notice requirements; require human review of all candidate-facing communications.

sections.heading: Code Safety & Secure Sandbox Execution
sections.description: All code generated by the LLM agent must be executed in an isolated sandbox environment with strict resource controls, preventing injection attacks, credential exposure, and unauthorized system access during iterative refinement cycles.

items.label: Enforce Isolated Sandbox with No Network Egress
items.description: Execute all AI-generated code in a containerized sandbox (e.g., gVisor, Firecracker microVM) with outbound network access fully disabled. Recruitment data must never be exfiltrated to external endpoints during test execution.

items.label: Scan Generated Code for Credential and PII Leakage
items.description: Run automated secret detection (e.g., TruffleHog, Gitleaks patterns) and PII regex scans on all generated code before execution to catch hardcoded API keys, database connection strings, SSNs, or candidate email addresses embedded in code literals.

items.label: Restrict Filesystem Access to Designated Temp Directories
items.description: Configure sandbox execution environments to mount only ephemeral, scoped temp directories. Generated code must not be permitted to read from or write to HR system directories, candidate databases, or configuration files outside the designated workspace.

items.label: Validate Dependency Manifests Against Approved Package Allowlist
items.description: Before executing generated code that imports third-party libraries, cross-reference all dependencies against a curated allowlist of vetted packages. Block execution if any package is unlisted, deprecated, or has known CVEs with CVSS score ≥ 7.0.

items.label: Enforce CPU, Memory, and Execution Time Limits
items.description: Apply hard resource caps per sandbox execution (e.g., 2 CPU cores, 512MB RAM, 30-second timeout) to prevent denial-of-service conditions from runaway generated code loops or recursive functions processing large candidate datasets.

items.label: Perform Static Syntax and Security Analysis Before Execution
items.description: Run generated code through a static analysis pipeline (e.g., Bandit for Python, ESLint security plugins for JS) to detect SQL injection patterns, eval() usage, unsafe deserialization, and shell injection vectors before any execution occurs.

items.label: Destroy Sandbox Instances and Ephemeral Data After Each Run
items.description: Ensure sandbox containers are torn down and all ephemeral storage is securely wiped after each code execution cycle. Candidate data loaded for test validation must not persist between agent iteration sessions.

items.label: Require Human Approval Gate Before Production Deployment of Generated Code
items.description: Mandate a qualified engineer review and sign-off on all AI-generated recruitment code before it is promoted to production environments, with approval logged and linked to the specific agent session and code version.

sections.heading: Candidate Transparency & Notification Requirements
sections.description: Comply with NYC Local Law 144, EU AI Act Article 13, and emerging state disclosure laws by ensuring candidates are proactively informed when AI-generated tools influence hiring decisions, with accessible opt-out or human review pathways.

items.label: Display AI Usage Disclosure at Application Entry Point
items.description: Implement a mandatory disclosure banner or modal at the start of every application flow stating that AI tools, including automated code-driven screening, are used in the hiring process. Disclosure must be in plain language and available in all languages supported by the application portal.

items.label: Provide Candidate-Facing Summary of AI Screening Criteria
items.description: Per NYC Local Law 144, publish a plain-language summary of the categories of data and the general methodology used by AI screening tools. This summary must be updated within 30 days of any material change to generated scoring logic.

items.label: Implement Human Review Request Mechanism
items.description: Build a clearly labeled 'Request Human Review' option into all AI-driven screening stages. The generated code handling this pathway must route the candidate to a human recruiter within 5 business days and log the request with timestamp.

items.label: Send Automated AI-Decision Notification on Adverse Outcomes
items.description: Trigger a notification email to candidates who are filtered out by AI-generated screening code, explicitly stating that an automated system was involved in the decision and providing contact information for the HR team to request reconsideration.

items.label: Maintain Candidate Consent Records for AI Processing
items.description: Store timestamped consent records for each candidate acknowledging AI use in their evaluation. Generated data pipeline code must reference and validate consent status before processing any candidate record through automated scoring functions.

items.label: Localize Disclosures for EU Candidates Under AI Act Requirements
items.description: For candidates in EU jurisdictions, generated application code must surface additional disclosures required under EU AI Act Article 13 for high-risk AI systems, including the system's intended purpose, accuracy metrics, and human oversight measures.

items.label: Archive Disclosure Delivery Logs for Regulatory Audit
items.description: Ensure all candidate disclosure events are logged with candidate ID, disclosure version, delivery timestamp, and acknowledgment status. Retain logs for 3 years to support NYC Local Law 144 audit requests and EEOC investigations.

sections.heading: Annual Bias Audits & Ongoing Model Governance
sections.description: Establish a continuous governance framework for AI-generated recruitment code, including mandatory annual bias audits, version control of generated models, and change management processes that satisfy NYC Local Law 144 and EU AI Act high-risk AI obligations.

items.label: Schedule and Document Annual Independent Bias Audits
items.description: Engage a qualified third-party auditor to conduct annual bias audits of all AI-generated screening and scoring code per NYC Local Law 144. Audits must test for disparate impact across race, sex, and intersectional categories, with results published on the company's public-facing careers page.

items.label: Maintain Version-Controlled Repository of All Generated Recruitment Code
items.description: Store every iteration of AI-generated recruitment code in a version-controlled repository (e.g., Git) with commit messages referencing the agent session ID, prompt version, and the business justification for each change. Enable rollback to any prior version within 1 hour.

items.label: Implement Drift Detection for Candidate Score Distributions
items.description: Deploy automated monitoring that tracks weekly distributions of candidate scores by demographic proxy signals. Alert the compliance team if score distributions shift by more than 5% for any protected group cohort between model versions or data refreshes.

items.label: Conduct Pre-Deployment Impact Assessment for Generated Code Changes
items.description: Before deploying any updated AI-generated screening logic, complete a structured Algorithmic Impact Assessment documenting: intended change, affected candidate populations, bias test results, and sign-off from both engineering and HR compliance leads.

items.label: Establish Incident Response Protocol for Discriminatory Output Detection
items.description: Define and document a formal incident response playbook for cases where AI-generated code produces discriminatory screening outcomes. Protocol must include: immediate suspension of the affected code, candidate re-review process, regulatory notification timeline, and root cause analysis within 72 hours.

items.label: Track and Report Adverse Impact Ratios Quarterly to HR Leadership
items.description: Generate automated quarterly reports from recruitment pipeline data showing selection rates by protected class at each AI-screened stage. Reports must be reviewed by HR leadership and retained as evidence of good-faith compliance efforts.

items.label: Validate Generated Code Against Updated Regulatory Requirements Annually
items.description: Assign a compliance owner to review all AI-generated recruitment code against the latest EEOC guidance, state hiring bias law updates, and EU AI Act implementing regulations each calendar year, with a documented gap analysis and remediation plan.

items.label: Require Retraining or Regeneration Triggers on Audit Failure
items.description: Define quantitative thresholds (e.g., adverse impact ratio below 0.80 for any protected group) that automatically trigger a mandatory regeneration of the affected code module by the AI agent, followed by full re-audit before redeployment.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: NYC Local Law 144 requires employers using AI-driven employment decision tools to conduct and publish annual bias audits by an independent third party before deploying such tools for NYC-based candidates. Non-compliance exposes employers to civil penalties of up to $1,500 per violation per day, and candidates must be notified of AI use at least 10 business days before any automated assessment.

The EU AI Act classifies AI systems used in recruitment, CV screening, and candidate evaluation as high-risk AI under Annex III. Deployers must maintain technical documentation, implement human oversight mechanisms, register the system in the EU AI database, and ensure generated code meets conformity assessment requirements before deployment to EU candidates.

EEOC Uniform Guidelines on Employee Selection Procedures require that any selection procedure—including AI-generated screening algorithms—be validated for job-relatedness and must not produce adverse impact (selection rate below 80% of the highest-selected group) against any race, sex, or ethnic group. Employers bear the burden of demonstrating validity if adverse impact is found.

The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) requires that AI-generated assessment and screening tools do not screen out qualified individuals with disabilities unless the criteria are job-related and consistent with business necessity. Generated code that sets cognitive, physical, or time-based thresholds must include documented reasonable accommodation pathways and must not rely on medical or disability-proximate data inputs.

cta.heading: Automate Your Recruitment/HR Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI-generated recruitment code for bias signals, sandbox violations, and regulatory drift—so your hiring pipeline stays compliant, auditable, and defensible without slowing down your engineering team.
